config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa pembatasan, mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan atas nama mayoritas. Di Indonesia, saya melihat kepentingan bersama memang penting, tapi harus dicapai lewat perlindungan hak dasar setiap orang, bukan dengan mengorbankannya. Masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk kalau individu merasa aman, dihargai, dan punya ruang untuk bersuara.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual’s rights. From a United States perspective, that kind of rule has too much potential to excuse abuse, because majorities and governments can always claim they are acting for the common good while trampling unpopular people. I do think individual rights are not absolute in every case, since laws against violence, fraud, and public health emergencies matter, but those limits should be narrow and carefully justified. In my view, a free society is strongest when it protects individual rights first and only restricts them when there is a clear, serious harm to others.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas, membungkam kritik, atau membenarkan kebijakan yang merugikan orang tertentu. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas supaya tidak berubah jadi alat pembenaran penyalahgunaan kuasa. Masyarakat yang kuat justru lahir ketika individu dilindungi, bukan ketika haknya dikorbankan atas nama mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my U.S. perspective, that framing is too dangerous because governments and majorities can always claim they are acting for the common good while ignoring minorities, dissenters, or unpopular people. I do think there are real exceptions, like public health rules or laws against harm, but those limits need to be narrow, temporary, and strongly justified. In my view, society is healthier when individual rights are the default and collective interests are balanced around them, not above them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus lebih dulu daripada hak individu secara umum. Bagi saya, pengalaman di Indonesia justru menunjukkan bahwa dalih “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk menekan minoritas, membatasi kritik, atau melegitimasi kebijakan yang tidak adil. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tegas; kalau tidak, mayoritas atau penguasa mudah sekali memakai label “masyarakat” untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Masyarakat yang sehat menurut saya bukan yang paling keras memaksa individu tunduk, melainkan yang paling mampu melindungi hak orang sambil tetap mencari solusi bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a United States perspective, that idea is too easy to abuse, because “the public good” has often been used to silence dissent, target minorities, or excuse overreach by the state. I do think there are limited cases where collective needs matter, like preventing direct harm or handling genuine emergencies, but those exceptions should be narrow and closely justified. To me, a healthy society is one that protects individual rights first and asks for collective sacrifice only when it is truly necessary and clearly limited.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
